mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month increases in industrial production indicate real economic expansion and rising corporate earnings, which drive positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: If INDPRO rises month-over-month for three consecutive months but SPX 60-day returns are negative or rank IC with INDPRO growth is below 0.05 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the unemployment rate below 6% signals that the labor market is healing after a recession, improving consumer confidence and spending, which supports positive equity returns over the following 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If after UNRATE falls below 6%, SPX 20-day returns are negative in more than 60% of triggered instances or average return is not significantly positive (t-test p>0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous positive three-month growth in retail sales and housing starts confirms a broad-based demand recovery, boosting corporate revenues and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: both RSAFS and HOUST show positive 3-month growth rate
regime_hint: economic recovery with synchronized demand pickup
failure_condition: If both series have positive 3-month growth but SPX 20-day returns are negative in > 50% of such months, or the joint signal fails to generate rank IC > 0.03 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: In a zero-lower-bound environment, a moderate rise in consumer prices signals reflation and improves nominal earnings expectations without immediate tightening risk, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the following 20 trading days.
regime_hint: zero lower bound with below-target inflation
failure_condition: If the trigger fires and SPX 20-day returns are negative more than half the time, or if CPIAUCSL growth > 0.1% with FEDFUNDS < 1% does not yield a positive mean return significantly different from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payrolls reflect strong labor demand and income growth, which fuels consumption and corporate profits, producing positive S&P 500 returns over a 20-day horizon without any additional trigger condition.
regime_hint: any regime except severe contraction (payrolls falling sharply)
failure_condition: If month-over-month PAYEMS increase is not followed by positive SPX 20-day returns in at least 55% of cases, or if the rank IC of PAYEMS changes with respect to 20-day returns is below 0.03 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts climbing above the 1,200 thousand mark signals a vigorous housing market that spills over into construction jobs and durable goods demand, boosting economic growth and equity valuations over the next quarter.
failure_condition: If HOUST crosses above 1200 but SPX 60-day returns are negative in the majority of such episodes, or the average return is not significantly above zero (t-test p>0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When unemployment remains above 5% but the Fed holds rates near zero, a decline in unemployment signals that accommodative policy is effectively boosting the labor market, which is very positive for short-term equity returns over the next week as risk appetite increases.
regime_hint: slack labor market with aggressive monetary accommodation
failure_condition: If under the trigger condition a month-over-month decline in UNRATE does not lead to positive SPX 5-day returns in at least 60% of occurrences, or if the interaction term has no significant predictive power in a linear model.

mechanism_narrative: Positive quarter-over-quarter real GDP growth indicates expanding economic activity, which normally lifts equities. However, the effect is conditional on the level of the federal funds rate: when the Fed is accommodative (FFR<2%), growth is unambiguously positive for forward 20-day returns, but when the Fed is tightening (FFR>2%) the same growth may trigger rate-hike fears and weigh on stocks.
evidence_refs: fact:Current GDPC1 16293.7 (annualized) showing growth from prior quarter
failure_condition: If positive GDP growth with FEDFUNDS < 2% produces negative SPX 20-day returns in more than 40% of cases, or if the conditional effect is indistinguishable from zero (interaction p>0.10 in regression).
